title: Plant species (higher), threatened
definition:
A threatened species is likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future, throughout all or a significant portion of its range. Threatened species are species classified by the IUCN as Critically Endangered (CR), Endangered (EN) and Vulnerable (VU).

Higher plants are native vascular plant species.

source.sourceOrganization:

International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN), uri: https://www.iucnredlist.org/

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Madagascar, Ecuador, Mexico, while the lightest include United Arab Emirates, Iceland, Kuwait; that contrast is the fastest way to read the threatened plant species pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Threatened plant species highlight biodiversity hotspots and island endemism in an expert-friendly way.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Threatened mammal species lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.
Threatened fish species can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Threatened bird species lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
editorial.dataCaveat: Raw species counts favor biodiverse and larger countries.
regionalSignals: Environment maps reward separating land cover, exposure, and emissions intensity.
Check both forested and arid or industrialized outliers.

reviewNotes: Batch 4 QA: strong plant-endemism and biodiversity pattern with excellent coverage.
Expert-only because the raw count and extreme outliers are advanced.
